description: Monorepo config: Dockerfiles, CI job images across .github/workflows/, tool pins in scripts and Makefiles. The waas-images repo (split out 2026-07-10) carries its own config again.

packageRules.description: Bump indirect Go deps too (off by Renovate default): the Trivy gate blocks main on HIGH/CRITICAL CVEs in go.mod, and x/net-style CVEs live in indirect deps.
One PR for all GitHub Actions patch/minor/digest bumps, automerged once required checks pass.
All non-major Go bumps (all four modules, direct and indirect) in one weekly PR, automerged once required checks pass and the release is >=10 days old.
Frontend devDependencies patch/minor bumps in one weekly PR, automerged: they never reach the shipped bundle, so a bad bump fails CI, not users.

Frontend production patch bumps in one weekly PR, automerged. Minors stay individual PRs under manual review: npm semver is declarative and the CI has no e2e, so a behavior-changing minor would go green unseen.
guacamole-common-js is protocol-coupled to guacd and the wwt tunnel: every bump gets its own PR and a manual review, never an automerge.
Container image digest/pin/patch bumps automerge once required checks pass — without this they pile up behind the concurrent-PR limit (the initial pin-dependencies PR sat rate-limited for a week).
Our own desktop images (docker.io/xorhub/*, built in the waas-images repo) release in lockstep with one shared version: every bump lands as one grouped PR instead of one per image.
xorhub images automerge through minor (not just patch/digest like third-party images): we cut those releases ourselves in waas-images, so a minor is already reviewed there.

customManagers.description: Container tools run via `docker run` in GitHub workflows and the root Makefile (gitleaks, kubeconform, hadolint — helm-check runs kubeconform locally too).

Workspace catalog and template images — official kasmweb ones and our own docker.io/xorhub desktop images (fixed release tags, optional @digest bumped together — the catalog matches templates verbatim).
k3s image booted by the k3d dev cluster (kept in sync with the k3d pin in .mise.toml — see the comment in k3d-config.yaml).
setup-envtest pin in operator/Makefile (ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is deliberate: it tracks the k8s.io/* minor in go.mod, not the latest).
tygo pin in the root Makefile (generated TS types).
helm-unittest pin in .mise.toml's postinstall hook (a helm plugin, not a [tools] entry — the built-in mise manager that bumps everything else in that file can't see it).
